_notes: NFL scheduling constraints derived from the NFL Constitution, CBA, and broadcast contracts.
Hard constraints are inviolable; soft constraints carry weighted penalties.
The NFL schedule is not round-robin: fixture generation uses a rotation formula.

hard.description: Each team plays exactly 17 regular-season games

hard.source: NFL scheduling formula — all 6 intra-division games are mandatory
hard.description: Each team plays home and away against each of its 3 division rivals

hard.source: NFL/NFLPA CBA — each team receives exactly one bye week between weeks 6-14
hard.description: Every team has exactly one bye week; no game that week
A team cannot have a bye in consecutive weeks (trivially satisfied with one bye)

hard.source: Stadium operations — MetLife (NYJ/NYG) and SoFi (LAC/LAR) cannot host both tenants same day
hard.description: Teams sharing a stadium cannot both play home games on the same day

hard.source: NFL tradition — Thanksgiving home teams (DAL, DET + 1 night game) are fixed annually
hard.description: Traditional Thanksgiving hosts (Dallas, Detroit) must play home on Thanksgiving

soft.source: NFL scheduling preference — player welfare and fan attendance
soft.description: Teams should not play more than 3 consecutive road games
Teams should not play more than 4 consecutive home games

soft.source: NFLPA CBA — Thursday Night Football team played Sunday road game = short turnaround
soft.description: Avoid scheduling a team on Thursday Night Football the week after a Sunday road game

soft.source: Broadcast contracts — all teams should appear in at least one prime-time slot
soft.description: Every team should have at least one SNF, MNF, or TNF appearance per season

soft.source: NFL competitive integrity — schedule formula ensures equivalent SOS across division rivals
soft.description: Teams in the same division face opponents of equivalent prior-year strength

soft.source: NFLPA CBA / team operations — minimize coast-to-coast consecutive trips
soft.description: Minimize consecutive long-haul road trips (e.g. East Coast team traveling to West Coast twice back-to-back)

soft.source: NFL scheduling standard — home/away split across first and second half of season
soft.description: Teams should have a balanced home/away split across the first and second half of the season

soft.source: NFL International Series rules — traveling teams get additional preparation time
soft.description: Teams playing international games (London, Munich, Brazil) should have adequate rest before and after

soft.source: NBC/NFL broadcast agreement — SNF flex scheduling requires viable matchup options in late season
soft.description: Weeks 5-18 should include at least one flex-eligible game per week for SNF broadcast

soft.source: NFL scheduling preference — spread division games across the season for sustained interest
soft.description: The two legs of each division rivalry should be at least 5 weeks apart
